name: harbor
title: Harbor Blvd (Fullerton → Santa Ana)
anchor_note: MEASURED (2026-07): OCTA quarterly performance reports give FY2019 route totals 43 = 2,095,510 and 543 = 953,471 (scripts/anchor_from_apc.py, source URLs inside). Weekday: 543 (wkdy-only) 3,739; 43 (7-day) 6,350-6,985; x FY2019->FY2024 system trend 0.90-0.99 (central 0.94); corridor = 543 + 43 x corridor share 0.75 (LODES) - 0.86 (ACS), scripts/route43_share.py. 43+543 = 8.3-8.7% of system FY2017-20, matching the TSP study's '8%' quote (which this data supersedes). Cross-check: 12,800 on-Harbor boardings in 2015 (Central Harbor study) x trend x street-vs-corridor share ~ 8,400.

rapid.note: Route 543 per corridor doc; GTFS current = 12.8 mph / 20 min (sensitivity row)

service_new.note: 5-min peak / 10-min off-peak operating plan (user decision 2026-07); flat 5-min all-day kept as sensitivity row. Base routes stay scalar: GTFS measures 43 and 543 at flat 20-min peak AND midday

visitor.note: Anaheim resort/visitor market: share of base boardings (pinned like tau); short trips (hotel-Disneyland-convention); random arrival (h/2 wait, no schedule adaptation). Judgment call: old first bin 0.55 split 0.25/0.30 into the new 0-0.5-mi bin -- visitor trips are exactly the sub-half-mile kind

bca.rev_hours_note: Weekday revenue vehicle-hours for the BCA's avoided base O&M (spec 06 E4): sum over one weekday's trips of (last-stop arrival - first-stop departure), OCTA GTFS in data/raw/gtfs. Weekday = the calendar.monday=1 services (both routes run their weekday trips under service_id MTUWTF): route 43 = 114 trips -> 183.3 h, route 543 = 86 trips -> 77.1 h. Fold sheds both the local 43 and the rapid 543; retain keeps the local 43 (only the 543 is folded into the new line).
